title: AI Doesn't Reduce Work—It Intensifies It
summary: UC Berkeley researchers tracked white-collar workers over a year and found that AI tools significantly increased output and task variety — but also hours worked, cognitive load, and burnout risk. Workers reported that AI didn't reduce their workload; it raised the ceiling on how much was expected of them.
why_it_matters: Longitudinal data founders need before scaling AI adoption — reframes 'efficiency' as 'sustainable performance' and gives Krissi a compelling hook for speaking on AI and workforce culture.

title: Research: How AI Is Changing the Labor Market
summary: HBR research synthesizes empirical findings on AI's net labor market effects, examining displacement, skills premiums, and hybrid human-AI workflow patterns. Entry-level white-collar roles face most structural pressure while demand surges for workers who can direct and evaluate AI output.
why_it_matters: Gives grounded data for founder conversations about whether to hire humans or AI agents for knowledge work roles in 2026.

title: Jack Dorsey Lays Off 40% of Block Because of AI
summary: Block cut 4,000 employees (40% of workforce) citing AI. Dorsey predicted most companies will make similar cuts within a year. Stock surged 24%. AI cited in 12,000+ US job cuts in 2026.
why_it_matters: First major tech CEO to publicly name AI as the driver of mass restructuring at this scale — makes this the defining data point for workforce strategy conversations.

title: Zuckerberg Is Poised to Finish What Dorsey Started: A Cascade of AI-Related Layoffs
summary: Meta weighing 20%+ layoffs (15,000+ roles) to offset $115-135B AI infrastructure spending. Analysts warn of cascade across tech sector.
why_it_matters: If Meta restructures at this scale and stock responds like Block's, it establishes a financial blueprint every public tech board will follow.

title: When Using AI Leads to Brain Fry
summary: BCG study: 14% of workers report cognitive exhaustion from AI tool overuse. Productivity peaks at 1–3 tools then drops. Manager support and work-life balance culture are the key mitigations.
why_it_matters: For founders rolling out AI tooling, this is the first rigorous data showing more AI tools ≠ more productivity, and the fix is managerial not technical.

title: Management as AI Superpower
summary: Mollick reports from an experimental Wharton course where student teams built startups in four days using AI. Management and coordination skills — not technical ability — were the differentiating factor. Teams with strong communicators outperformed technically stronger teams.
why_it_matters: A powerful counter-narrative for Krissi's talks: the AI era doesn't diminish the value of great leadership hiring — it amplifies it.

title: AI Has Made Hiring Worse — But It Can Still Help
summary: First wave of AI in hiring created an automation arms race making the process more inhumane and less predictive. Framework offered: use AI selectively at assessment and onboarding stages rather than sourcing to improve outcomes.
why_it_matters: Validates contrarian take for talks: AI in hiring made things worse before better, and selective deployment is the winning pattern.

title: Companies Are Laying Off Workers Because of AI's Potential — Not Its Performance
summary: Organizations are cutting headcount based on anticipated AI capabilities rather than demonstrated performance. Gartner finds only 1 in 50 AI investments delivers transformational value, yet layoffs tied to AI expectations are accelerating. Several companies quietly backfilled after AI underdelivered.
why_it_matters: Gives founders data to resist competitor-pressure layoffs and positions you as the advisor who prevents costly mistakes.

title: Is Block's Decision to Lay Off 40% of Its Workforce a Bellwether — or Not?
summary: Bersin's analysis of 70+ companies finds that productivity-tool AI rarely eliminates jobs — real cuts require workflow re-engineering. Block's 40% cut likely reflects organizational bloat as much as AI efficiency.
why_it_matters: Most credible pushback on the Dorsey narrative — helps distinguish genuine AI-driven restructuring from cost-cutting dressed as transformation.

title: Three Years from GPT-3 to Gemini 3
summary: Mollick reflects on the shift from chatbot to digital coworker era. 'Human in the loop' is evolving from 'human who fixes AI mistakes' to 'human who directs AI work.' Uses Gemini 3 as the milestone.
why_it_matters: 'Digital coworker' is the most usable framing for explaining the capability shift to non-technical audiences.

title: Why AI-Layoff Stories Are Misleading
summary: Bersin argues most AI-layoff headlines conflate cost-cutting with transformation. Real AI-enabled workforce redesign looks different from Block's announcement. Covers Oracle, Workday, Microsoft misreadings.
why_it_matters: Essential for advising before board conversations about Block-style cuts.

title: The Great Reinvention of Human Resources Has Begun
summary: Bersin argues 2026 is when AI starts fundamentally restructuring HR: 30–40% of HR roles automated by AI agents, HR BPs must evolve into strategic advisors, full-stack HR model.
why_it_matters: Clearest current map for startup founders on how to build their People function in an AI-first world — challenges linear HR headcount scaling assumptions.

title: How AI Skills and Experience Are Transforming the Workplace
summary: WEF analysis of all US job postings 2019-2025: automation-prone roles fell 13% after ChatGPT; analytical/technical roles grew 20%. Workers with AI skills earn up to 56% wage premium.
why_it_matters: Empirical counterbalance to Block/Meta headlines — labor market is bifurcating, not collapsing.

title: Is AI the Strategy — or the Scapegoat — Behind Block's 40% Layoff?
summary: Darden faculty examine whether Block's restructuring is genuine AI efficiency or AI-as-cover for financially-driven cuts. Argues the two motivations are not mutually exclusive.
why_it_matters: Gives the critical-thinking frame for fielding 'should we be cutting like Block?' from board members and founders.

title: Land Your Dream Job in Today's Market: Phyl Terry on Job Search Councils and Negotiation
summary: Phyl Terry of Never Search Alone covers Job Search Councils (6–8 person peer groups), candidate-market fit, listening tours, and principled offer negotiation for AI-disrupted job markets.
why_it_matters: Useful for coaching conversations with both job-seekers in your network and startup clients evaluating how candidates are showing up in their pipelines.

title: TA Trends 2026: Human–AI Power Couple
summary: Major annual TA research: 84% of talent leaders will use AI in hiring in 2026, 52% plan to add autonomous AI agents as team members. Human-AI hybrid orgs outperform on time-to-hire and candidate quality. Candidate fraud and AI-generated applications are top threats.
why_it_matters: Benchmark data for advising startup founders on what their TA function should look like in 2026.

title: GPT-5: It Just Does Stuff
summary: Mollick's first-person assessment of GPT-5: the model proactively generates deliverables you didn't request. Prompted for startup ideas, it returned landing pages, LinkedIn copy, and a financial model unprompted. The bottleneck has shifted from 'how to produce' to 'knowing what to ask for.'
why_it_matters: Best current evidence that AI has crossed a threshold from assistant to autonomous collaborator — essential framing for talks on what AI-era hiring and leadership looks like.

title: Why LinkedIn Is Replacing PMs with AI-Powered "Full-Stack Builders"
summary: LinkedIn CPO Tomer Cohen scrapped the APM program and replaced it with an Associate Product Builder track. New hires learn coding, design, and PM skills together. Full Stack Builder is now a formal title and career ladder.
why_it_matters: Most concrete real-world case study of a major tech company redesigning its talent model for the AI era — directly usable when advising AI startup founders on org structure.

title: Why LinkedIn Is Turning PMs Into AI-Powered "Full Stack Builders" | Tomer Cohen
summary: Full interview with LinkedIn CPO Tomer Cohen on killing the APM program, building the Full Stack Builder track, and redesigning LinkedIn's product org. Cohen argues unteachable-by-AI skills — vision, empathy, creativity, judgment — are what to cultivate.
why_it_matters: Most substantive conversation on rebuilding a product org for AI era — essential before advising Series B+ startup CPOs on team design.

title: GPT-5 and the New Era of Work
summary: OpenAI's framing of how GPT-5.4 changes professional work. Matches/exceeds professionals in 83% of knowledge work benchmarks, supports 1M token context, first general-purpose model with native computer-use. Enterprise users save 40-60 min/day on average.
why_it_matters: Authoritative baseline for what clients are buying into — critical for calibrating expectations on productivity timelines and which roles to hire vs. automate.

title: AI at Your Jobs
summary: Roose and Newton examine the gap between executive AI enthusiasm and front-line employee experience. Wrestles with whether products designed to eliminate jobs should be more transparent about that purpose.
why_it_matters: Captures the ethical and human dimension of AI workforce transition — useful for opening a talk that acknowledges employee anxiety authentically.

title: Make Product Management Fun Again with AI Agents
summary: Practical guide for PMs using AI agents to automate user research synthesis, competitive analysis, and spec writing. Includes a framework for knowing which PM tasks to automate vs. protect as human judgment.
why_it_matters: Useful playbook for startup clients whose first PM hire must do the work of three — sets realistic expectations for AI-augmented product roles.

title: When Using AI Leads to 'Brain Fry'
summary: A new HBR study explores cognitive overload among heavy AI users, identifying context-switching between AI-generated content and original thinking as the main driver of 'brain fry'. The research offers specific mitigation strategies for managers.
why_it_matters: Gives Krissi a concrete framework for advising early-stage AI startups on structuring AI-augmented workflows that prevent early-team burnout.

title: We Replaced Our Sales Team With 20 AI Agents—Here's What Happened Next
summary: Jason Lemkin replaced SaaStr's entire GTM team with 20 AI agents managed by 1.2 humans. Agents handle outbound, inbound, and follow-up 24/7. Candid account of what broke, what worked.
why_it_matters: Most concrete real-world example of deliberate AI workforce replacement—essential before conversations with founders considering first sales hire.

title: AI Isn't Reducing Workloads—It's Straining Employees
summary: An ActivTrak analysis of 10,584 workers found that since adopting AI, time spent emailing doubled and deep focus work sessions fell by 9%. Workers are spending more cognitive energy coordinating with and reviewing AI outputs rather than doing their core work.
why_it_matters: Counternarrative to 'AI will free up my team'—useful for advising founders on realistic AI adoption planning and avoiding productivity theater.

title: Gartner: The Top Four Talent Acquisition Trends for 2026
summary: Gartner identifies AI-first hiring, recruiter role evolution toward strategic advisory, early-career pipeline redesign, and AI proficiency assessment as the four forces reshaping TA in 2026. By 2027, 75% of hiring processes will include formal AI skills certification.
why_it_matters: Foundational data for advising TA leaders on where to invest recruiting capability and how to evolve recruiter roles.

title: From Context Engineers to Chief AI Officers: Emerging AI Job Roles for 2026
summary: Details six AI-native roles gaining traction: Context Engineers, RAG Engineers (£110k–£180k), AI Governance Directors, AI Operations Managers, AI GTM Engineers, and Chief AI Officers. Each role defined with responsibilities and salary benchmarks.
why_it_matters: Gives startup founders concrete role definitions when building their first AI-native org chart.

title: AI Talent vs. AI Tools: What Early-Stage Startups Should Actually Hire For in 2026
summary: A practical framework distinguishing between building internal AI capability versus leveraging AI tools to amplify a lean generalist team. Most seed-stage companies default to over-hiring for AI expertise when off-the-shelf tooling would serve them better.
why_it_matters: A question Krissi gets from founders constantly — a well-reasoned framework she can adapt and cite directly in client conversations.

title: Why Gen AI Feels So Threatening to Workers
summary: HBR identifies three distinct AI threat types affecting workers: threat to identity, job security, and mastery. Workers experiencing identity threat show higher disengagement even when their jobs are safe.
why_it_matters: Helps founders understand which threat type employees are experiencing to communicate and manage AI transitions effectively.

title: The Shape of the Thing
summary: Mollick argues we've entered a new era where AI agents handle multi-hour tasks autonomously. The human role has shifted from doing work alongside AI to managing AI that does the work — changing what good management looks like.
why_it_matters: The managing-AI-vs-working-with-AI reframe is key mental model shift for startup leadership teams and strong speaking material.

title: Why AI Demands a New Breed of Leaders
summary: MIT Sloan article by Hoque, Davenport & Nelson (April 2025) arguing that CIOs and CTOs lack both the bandwidth and mandate to address the cultural and organizational dimensions of AI transformation. The authors propose a new C-suite role — Chief Innovation and Transformation Officer (CITO) — that combines technical expertise with organizational psychology, change management, and ethical oversight. Key data: 85% of IT leaders see CIOs as changemakers but only 28% call transformation their top priority; 91% of large-company data leaders cite cultural/change management as the #1 impediment to AI success (not technology). Real examples include PepsiCo (Athina Kanioura as Chief Strategy & Transformation Officer), Standard Chartered Bank (Roel Louwhoff), JPMorgan Chase (Teresa Heitsenrether on AI governance), and TIAA (Sastry Durvasula overseeing 60% of employees across org, tech, and operational change). New section on AI Persona Management: as agentic AI grows, companies need leaders who can manage AI agents with defined identities, decision authority, and ethical boundaries — Salesforce already uses AI personas internally to guide product decisions. BCG study: companies hiring Chief Transformation Officers increased 140% from 2019-2021, with significant increase in shareholder return in the year after appointment.
why_it_matters: The CITO framework and the 91% cultural-barrier stat are directly usable in talks on AI leadership hiring — and the AI Persona Management section is a sharp, underreported angle on an emerging new job function.

title: The Last Mile Problem Slowing AI Transformation
summary: The biggest obstacle to AI transformation is embedding AI into actual human workflows. AI pilots succeed but org-wide rollouts stall due to integration complexity, change resistance, and the demo-vs-adoption gap. Authors propose a last mile playbook treating adoption as behavior change, not tooling.
why_it_matters: Gives a concrete framework for advising on AI adoption strategy, including the specific roles responsible for bridging the last mile gap.

title: AI Unlocks New Power for Employees: Are HR Leaders Ready?
source: Josh Bersin / MIT Sloan Management Review
summary: Bersin argues AI creates superemployees who can operate at team-level performance individually. Most HR systems are still designed for old individual-job performance model. Calls for fundamental redesign of L&D, performance management, and compensation around the superemployee paradigm.
why_it_matters: Startup clients building lean AI-augmented teams likely have outdated HR infrastructure — this gives you a framework and vocabulary for that conversation.

title: Hard Fork Ep. 183: Anton Korinek on Ghost GDP and AI Labor Markets
summary: Economist Anton Korinek introduces Ghost GDP concept — value created by AI that existing metrics can't yet measure — explaining why there's a disconnect between AI's technical progress and observable economic impact. Nuanced take on why mass job displacement hasn't materialized on predicted timeline.
why_it_matters: Ghost GDP framing makes you sharper in founder/investor conversations about whether to hire or automate.

title: Five Trends in AI and Data Science for 2026
summary: Davenport and Bean identify five 2026 trends: agentic AI, governance focus, AI-as-colleague shift, growing human oversight roles, and domain-specific models. Organizations with strong AI governance are now pulling ahead of fast-movers without guardrails.
why_it_matters: Human oversight roles is a new job family you can name and spec for clients, and governance-wins-long-term counters move-fast pressure founders face.

title: No Priors: Jensen Huang on AI, Productivity, and Whether Robots Take Jobs
summary: Jensen Huang argues AI will increase productivity without necessarily displacing workers, and that physical AI addresses labor shortages. Covers biggest surprises of 2025 and profitability of inference tokens entering 2026.
why_it_matters: Jensen's employment framing is shaping how top founders and investors think about workforce strategy — knowing it helps you push back intelligently.

title: Giving Your AI a Job Interview
summary: Mollick proposes treating AI model selection like a hiring decision, with structured tests for reasoning, creativity, accuracy, and domain fit. Includes a practical evaluation protocol teams can run in under an hour.
why_it_matters: A framework Krissi can hand directly to founders who ask 'which AI tools should my team actually be using?' — turns a confusing vendor landscape into a structured decision process.

title: 9 Trends Shaping Work in 2026 and Beyond
summary: HBR synthesis of nine forces reshaping work: skills-based hiring, AI agent teams, declining trust in AI tools despite rising adoption, and collapse of entry-level hiring pipelines. Flags premature layoffs and cultural dissonance as top avoidable failure modes.
why_it_matters: Credible HBR source to anchor conversations with founders tempted to cut headcount ahead of AI capability actually materializing.

title: ChatGPT Agent Mode: The Little Helper That Transformed Recruiting
summary: HoneyBook practitioner case study on deploying ChatGPT agent mode for LinkedIn recruiting. Autonomous system surfaced missed candidates and handled outreach. Documents failure modes and workflow adjustments required for reliability.
why_it_matters: Real-world implementation story more actionable than vendor case studies — shows what agentic recruiting looks like when a small team deploys it.

title: 2026 Imperatives: The Superworker Organization
summary: Bersin's annual imperatives introduce the Superworker Organization model: AI superagents handle end-to-end HR workflows while humans focus on judgment and managing agents. New roles emerge including AI Workflow Architect and People Data Strategist.
why_it_matters: Gives vocabulary for advising founders on structuring people function at scale, with concrete new role names for hiring conversations.

title: ReThinking: Margaret Atwood on What AI Can't Replace
summary: Adam Grant and Margaret Atwood examine which creative and intellectual capabilities AI will and won't replace, with Atwood rigorous about what creativity actually requires. Grant brings org psychology lens on valuing human talent alongside AI.
why_it_matters: Credible nuanced version of the protect-human-judgment argument for founder conversations about what to hire for vs. automate.

title: The 2026 AI Forecast: Foundation Models, IPOs, and Robotics
summary: Annual forecast episode on foundation model trajectory, IPO-ready AI companies, and robotics intersection with knowledge work hiring. AI predicted to shift from reactive tools to proactive agents. Includes their view on defensible startup team structures.
why_it_matters: The VC perspective on AI-era team building that startup clients are hearing from investors — helps you speak the same language.

title: 'Engineer' is so 2025. In AI land, everyone's a 'builder' now
summary: Reported from inside AI companies including Walmart, this piece tracks the emergence of 'builder' as a new cross-functional role identity — nontechnical employees using AI agents to ship products without writing code. All Walmart agent builder roles were filled internally by people who voluntarily retrained.
why_it_matters: Fresh evidence (March 5) that new job families are forming around AI agency rather than engineering credentials — directly useful when advising founders on early team titles and internal mobility programs.

title: Global Talent Trends 2026
summary: Mercer's annual flagship research surveys thousands of executives globally and finds companies combining human and AI capabilities gain measurable competitive advantage. The 2026 report identifies 'human-AI teaming' as the defining workforce design challenge.
why_it_matters: A go-to citation for speaking engagements — Mercer data carries board-level credibility and the human-AI teaming framework maps directly to what early AI startup founders ask Krissi about.

title: 2026 Global Human Capital Trends
summary: Deloitte's annual human capital research identifies the tension between AI-driven productivity gains and organizational readiness as the defining 2026 challenge. Introduces a 'human-AI ROI' measurement framework.
why_it_matters: The human-AI ROI framework gives Krissi boardroom-ready language for conversations with founders who need to justify team design decisions to investors.

title: The Talent Acquisition Revolution: How AI Is Transforming Recruiting
summary: Bersin's major research study maps how AI is reshaping every stage of talent acquisition from sourcing and screening to interviewing and onboarding. Leading companies are using AI to dramatically compress time-to-hire while improving quality.
why_it_matters: The most comprehensive current map of the AI recruiting tools landscape — essential reading when Krissi advises startup founders on which parts of TA to automate first.

title: From Context Engineers to Chief AI Officers: Emerging AI Job Roles for 2026
summary: A comprehensive taxonomy of new AI-specific job families including Context Engineers, RAG Engineers, Director of AI Governance & Risk, and Chief AI Officers, with salary ranges and hiring demand signals for each role.
why_it_matters: A practical cheat sheet for Krissi when a startup founder asks 'what roles should I actually be hiring for?' — defensible taxonomy with market comps.

title: Building AI Product Sense, Part 2
summary: Marily Nika outlines a weekly practice for rapidly developing AI product intuition — including how to evaluate AI outputs critically, when to override model suggestions, and how to build taste for 'good' AI-generated work.
why_it_matters: For Krissi advising founders on hiring their first AI PM, this piece defines what 'AI product sense' actually looks like in practice — making it easier to screen for the trait in interviews.
